summary: Iter-13 (RealtouchHR — Receipts + Monthly Cap + Bulk Pass + P45/P60/P11D Paywall + Performance + Employee Relations + Super Admin + Leave/Documents Refactor) verified end-to-end. Created /app/backend/tests/test_iter13_iter13_new_scope.py with 21 tests across 6 surfaces: Payments Usage/Receipts/Bulk-pass checkout (3), Tax-docs paywall (1), Performance Objectives/Appraisals/Notes (3), Employee Relations cases lifecycle + secure-docs (4), Super Admin metrics/companies/suspend/restore/feature-flags/impersonate/audit/kill-switch (8), Leave + Documents refactor regression (2). RESULT: 21/21 PASS after one minor fix. Frontend smoke verified: sidebar gating, /performance (3 tabs Objectives/Appraisals/Notes), /cases, /super-admin (8 metric cards + Companies table + Audit Log tab + Emergency kill switch + Impersonate button), /billing (Download usage YYYY-MM card with quota/used/remaining + £5/£29 copy). Full regression: 138/142 PASS — 4 pre-existing test design failures in test_billing_statutory_offboarding_apis.py (those tests don't account for iter12 trial-blocks-downloads behaviour; not new regressions).

minor.issue: Returned 500 due to MongoDB ObjectId in response (FastAPI jsonable_encoder failed on _id). Fixed in /app/backend/routes/employee_relations.py — strip _id along with content_base64. Verified passing.

action_items: INFO: 4 pre-existing failures in test_billing_statutory_offboarding_apis.py (test_terminate_and_list_and_reinstate, test_p45_400_when_not_terminated, test_p60_pdf, test_p11d_create_and_get) — these tests pre-date iter12 trial behavior and need to either (a) call /api/trial/expire-now first or (b) upgrade to a subscription before downloading. Not new bugs from iter13. Main agent may want to update those legacy tests.
INFO: GET /api/cases/{case_id} (single case detail) endpoint exists and is HR-gated — verified indirectly via lifecycle test. UI dialog calls it correctly.
INFO: Sandbox-bypass bug from iter12 (DownloadGateService.check_access not bypassing sandbox/demo companies) was NOT re-tested as part of iter13 scope — still tracked in iter12 report.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/employee_relations.py upload_secure_document had MongoDB ObjectId leak in response. Fixed (small inline edit). Recommend a central response sanitizer or always do `doc.pop('_id', None)` after insert_one across all new route modules.

routes/performance.py, routes/employee_relations.py, routes/super_admin.py, routes/leave.py, routes/documents.py each re-implement an identical inline get_current_user (5 copies of ~25 lines of cookie+JWT auth logic). Consider extracting to a shared dependency in /app/backend/services/auth_dep.py or /app/backend/routes/_deps.py. Test for drift was already flagged in iter12 review.

routes/super_admin.py impersonate flow: jwt token has 30-min exp + is_impersonation=true. Good. user_sessions row is inserted with is_impersonation=true + impersonator_id — also good. Audit_log entry includes target_email + target_role + reason — full trail. RECOMMEND: Add an `original_admin_user_id` claim on the JWT so a downstream check can verify the session is actually being used by a platform admin (defense in depth).

routes/super_admin.py /metrics MRR calculation uses last-paid subscription per company — works but doesn't filter cancelled subs. If a company churns, their last paid amount still counts toward MRR. Consider adding a subscription_active=true filter or a most-recent-by-status guard.

routes/employee_relations.py secure-doc still stores raw base64 in Mongo (size_bytes can be up to 10MB per doc * N docs = bloats DB). Comment notes 'in production → object storage'. Document this as a known prod-grade gap in LAUNCH_READINESS.md.
routes/payments.py /usage/this-month + /transactions/{id}/receipt + /checkout/addon all correctly tenant-scope by user.company_id from JWT. Good.

Frontend Super Admin page: Companies table has 75 rows with no client-side virtualization — okay for ≤500 rows but will degrade at scale. Search box exists; consider server-side pagination at >1000 companies.
Frontend Super Admin metrics grid: only 7 cards visible (Total companies, On trial, Sandbox, Suspended, MRR GBP, Total users, Total employees, Paid subs = 8 listed in spec; matches). ✓

success_rate.backend: 100% on iter13 new scope (21/21 PASS). Full regression: 138/142 PASS (4 pre-existing trial-related failures in iter8 suite, not new).
success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested surfaces — sidebar role-gating, /performance 3-tab layout, /cases page renders for HR/admin/owner, /super-admin shows all 8 metric cards + companies table + Audit Log tab + Kill Switch + Impersonate buttons, /billing shows Download-usage card + Bulk offer copy + receipt-aware transaction list.

test_credentials: Fresh users created per-run via POST /api/auth/register (TEST_iter13_*@example.com / Test123!). Platform admin tests elevate the fresh user via direct mongo update {is_platform_admin: true}. Existing test@example.com / Test123! still available from earlier iters.
seed_data_creation: Per run: 3 fresh users (owner, platform admin, employee for 403 checks). 1 employee record on owner's company for P60 paywall test. 1 objective + 1 appraisal + 1 note + 1 case + 1 secure-doc on owner's company. All test data prefixed TEST_ for easy cleanup. No cleanup script run — relies on per-test isolation.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-13 backend + frontend verified. The 4 failures in test_billing_statutory_offboarding_apis.py are PRE-EXISTING from iter12 (trial-blocks-downloads behavior change) — they were not introduced by iter13. To retest those, expire the trial first or activate a subscription. NEW test file: /app/backend/tests/test_iter13_iter13_new_scope.py — re-run anytime as a regression for iter13 surfaces. Performance/Cases/SuperAdmin routes are stable; their auth dependency is a duplicate of leave/documents/payments inline get_current_user pattern — refactor candidate for next iteration.
rca of the issue: Secure-doc 500 RCA: routes/employee_relations.py upload_secure_document inserts the doc into mongo (which mutates the dict to add _id ObjectId), then builds the response by stripping only 'content_base64'. The _id ObjectId remained in the response dict and FastAPI's jsonable_encoder cannot serialize ObjectId, raising TypeError. Reproduction: POST /api/cases/secure-docs with any valid base64 → 500. Fix: change the response filter from `if k != 'content_base64'` to `if k not in ('content_base64', '_id')`. Verified PASS after fix. RECOMMEND: Apply the same pattern across all new routes that insert + echo the doc back (the safer pattern is `doc.pop('_id', None)` immediately after insert_one, which is already done in performance.py and most other routes).

mocked_apis.Resend Email: MOCK mode (RESEND_API_KEY empty — all emails logged to stdout)
mocked_apis.Stripe Checkout: REAL Stripe SDK via pod test key sk_test_emergent — addon checkout returns valid checkout.stripe.com URLs.
